facts.claim: Multi-tenant hierarchy: Empresa (company) → Estabelecimento (establishment) → Setor (sector) → Leito (bed); scoped by middleware chain (EmpresaMiddleware → EstabelecimentoMiddleware → SetorMiddleware → LeitoMiddleware) resolving tenant context into request.empresa/estabelecimento/setor/leito

Processing mode (tipo) enumeration gates pathway selection: tipo='manual' routes to Movimentacao trilhas; tipo='automatica' to Leito.get_total_alertas_automaticos; tipo='homecare' to Leito.get_total_assistidos_homecare; branching implemented across occupancy, gender, alert, and assisted-patient counts

Occupancy formula at establishment and sector scopes: (occupied/total)×100; sector-level has ativo/ocupado asymmetry (denominator filtered by ativo=True, numerator by ocupado=True only), allowing occupancy >100% if inactive beds marked ocupado=True, violating WHO BOR definition

Alert/gender/assisted aggregation merges two sources: (1) manual Movimentacao records, (2) automatic-pathway Leito aggregates; summed per alert level (VERMELHO/AMARELO/NEUTRO) and gender (M/F/N='não informado'/O)

Macro-indicator aggregation divergence: EstabelecimentoStatusSerializer uses Sum/Avg aggregate with Round(precision=2); SetorStatusSerializer uses single-record .get() with no rounding—materially different implementations of identical metric (vidas_salvas, obitos, tempo_permanencia, tx_mortalidade, tx_ocupacao, admissao)

Sector indicadores aggregates VERMELHO/AMARELO/NEUTRO counts across every care-pathway type (trilha_homecare, trilha_automatica); homecare uses DISTINCT ON max(criado_em) per patient/atendimento; automatica assumes trilha pk==nr_atendimento; risk of TypeError if Sum() returns None on empty queryset

load_bearing_rules.summary: Processing-mode (tipo) enumeration gates pathway: manual/automatica/homecare select distinct alert/gender/assisted calculation branches
EmpresaMiddleware path-based tenant resolution: scans URL segments, resolves empresa FK, silently swallows all exceptions via bare except
EstabelecimentoMiddleware adds cross-check: if request.empresa already set, must match resolved estabelecimento.empresa, else HTTP 400
SetorMiddleware validates setor's estabelecimento against both request.empresa and request.estabelecimento; dual cross-check rejects mismatches with HTTP 400
Establishment occupancy ativo/ocupado asymmetry: total_leitos filtered ativo=True, total_leitos_ocupados filtered ocupado=True only (no ativo), can exceed 100%
Sector occupancy ativo/ocupado asymmetry: denominator filters ativo=True, numerator filters ocupado=True only, can exceed 100%
Sector indicadores aggregates VERMELHO/AMARELO/NEUTRO across trilha models; homecare picks DISTINCT ON max(criado_em) per atendimento; assumes automatica pk==nr_atendimento
Company-wide indicadores scopes to user's establishments/sectors: filters movimentacoes by atual=True, leito__ocupado=True, setor__usuarios=user
Establishment indicadores scopes setores to user-membership; returns SetorStatusSerializer data ordered by sector name
SetorViewSet.get_queryset applies dual independent scoping (AND): filters by both request.empresa and request.estabelecimento if present
GrupoAcesso exactly-one-scope constraint (three_xor): must attach to exactly one of empresa/estabelecimento/setor, not zero or 2+
LeitoMiddleware path-based bed resolution without cross-tenant check; no validation that resolved bed belongs to expected empresa/estabelecimento/setor context

systemic_issues.title: ativo/ocupado scoping asymmetry across occupancy formulas
systemic_issues.detail: Both establishment and sector (occupied/total)×100 formulas: total_leitos filters ativo=True but total_leitos_ocupados filters ocupado=True without ativo guard. If any bed has ocupado=True but ativo=False, numerator exceeds denominator, pushing occupancy >100%. Violates WHO BOR definition (occupied is strict subset of available).

systemic_issues.title: Code duplication across establishment/sector serializers
systemic_issues.detail: Establishment/sector gender/alert/assisted totals implement structurally identical tipo-branching (manual vs automatica/homecare) separately in EstabelecimentoStatusSerializer and SetorStatusSerializer. Macro-indicator fetch uses different implementations (aggregate with Round vs single-record .get()). No shared helper; code copy-pasted.

systemic_issues.title: Silent exception handling in tenant resolution and data fetches
systemic_issues.detail: Bare except: / except Exception: pass used in macro-indicator fetch (SetorStatusSerializer) and all 4 tenant-resolution middlewares; swallows DoesNotExist, IndexError, UUID errors, MultipleObjectsReturned with no logging. Middleware bare except leaves request.setor/leito unset; macro-indicator returns empty dict silently.

systemic_issues.title: Commented-out ocupado filters in aggregation queries
systemic_issues.detail: SetorStatusSerializer.get_total_generos/get_total_alertas and EmpresaViewSet.indicadores have 'ocupado=True' filters commented out; all active beds (occupied or not) count toward gender/alert totals. Inconsistent vs manual branch which counts only current occupants via Movimentacao.atual=True.

systemic_issues.title: Missing cross-tenant validation in LeitoMiddleware
systemic_issues.detail: EmpresaMiddleware, EstabelecimentoMiddleware, SetorMiddleware all perform cross-checks (HTTP 400 on mismatch); LeitoMiddleware has no cross-check against request.setor/estabelecimento/empresa. No prefetch_related, no validation that resolved bed belongs to expected tenant context.

systemic_issues.title: Implicit field-ordering dependency in serializer
systemic_issues.detail: SetorSerializer.get_generos relies on self.generos_automaticos populated by get_alertas (must run first). No explicit safeguard; depends on DRF field declaration order (alertas before generos). If order changes, AttributeError.

systemic_issues.title: TypeError risk in null-handling for aggregate queries
systemic_issues.detail: SetorViewSet.indicadores: Sum() over empty queryset returns None (not 0). Code uses .get(key, 0) as default only for MISSING dict key, not for None value present. If trilha has zero matching rows, '+= None' raises TypeError. Manifests when care pathway has no records for occupied beds.

systemic_issues.title: Error message wording mismatch (entity name confusion)
systemic_issues.detail: SetorViewSet.destroy() raises ValidationError 'Você não pode apagar um leito com ocupações!' (cannot delete a bed with occupations) when deleting setor. Message incorrectly names 'leito' instead of 'setor'. Copied from LeitoViewSet.destroy() without adaptation; EstabelecimentoViewSet.destroy() correctly says 'estabelecimento'.

systemic_issues.title: Silent logo data loss on type mismatch
systemic_issues.detail: EmpresaViewSet.manage_data: if 'logo' field present but not a string (e.g. dict, file object), it is silently popped and discarded with no logo_b64 set and no error. Non-string logo values lost without client feedback.

systemic_issues.title: Uncorroborated pk==nr_atendimento assumption in automatica pathway
systemic_issues.detail: SetorViewSet.indicadores assumes for automatica pathways that trilha model's primary key IS the nr_atendimento value itself. Cannot be independently confirmed from this partition; direct reading of code but comment/documentation missing.

v2_domain_detail.platform: Multi-tenant data isolation via path-based middleware chain; request context population (empresa/estabelecimento/setor/leito); queryset filtering by tenant scope; cross-tenant validation guards
v2_domain_detail.governance: Permission/access control: GrupoAcesso tenant scoping (exactly-one constraint), can_manage_empresa permission gating (GET exempted), proprietario lifecycle management, visibility gating by company tipo (homecare-only buttons)
v2_domain_detail.experience: Dashboard auto-refresh polling (empresa.tempo_atualizacao in seconds); chat preview last-message selection; sector picker cascading enable/disable; field-level exposure gating (EstabelecimentoSerializer camera credentials only on retrieve/partial_update)

tipo.description: Processing mode enumeration; controls which pathway engine and alert logic apply
tipo.notes: Empty string = 'Não informado'; Empresa defaults to ''; Estabelecimento/Setor/Leito default to 'manual'

alerta.description: Alert severity level; used in Movimentacao and trilha records
alerta.notes: Aggregated as counts in occupancy views and indicadores

genero.notes: N = 'não informado'; O likely 'outro'; enumerations not HL7/FHIR conformant

assistido.description: Flag indicating patient has been attended/assisted; aggregated per sector

whitelabel.description: Company brand identifier, unique across all companies
whitelabel.notes: Enforced unique=True at DB level; supports multiple white-labeled deployments

proprietario.description: Company owner (Usuario FK); save() ensures owner has 'p' access code, revokes from previous owner if they own no other company

tempo_atualizacao.description: Dashboard auto-refresh interval in seconds; default 300; multiplied by 1000 for JS setInterval milliseconds
tempo_atualizacao.notes: PositiveIntegerField; drives polling in empresa and estabelecimento dashboards

cor_primaria.description: Company primary color; must be exactly 6 characters (hex without #)

examples: Você não pode apagar um estabelecimento com leitos ocupados! (correct entity name)
Você não pode apagar um leito com ocupações! (DISCREPANCY: wrong entity name used for setor deletion)

open_questions: Is the ativo/ocupado asymmetry in occupancy formulas (003, 004) intentional, or a drift from WHO BOR standard?
Should commented-out ocupado=True filters in aggregation queries (034, 035, 041) be removed or restored?
Can SetorSerializer.get_generos dependency on alertas field ordering (013) be made explicit or refactored?
Is the pk==nr_atendimento assumption for automatica trilha models (038) documented/enforced anywhere?
